interactivity.rationale: Figure B provides more meaningful interactions, including an additional slider for the target pixel position and clickable elements that trigger smooth step-through animations.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure A accurately reproduces the layout, colors, dashed lines, and labeled curved arrows of the original, whereas Figure B is poorly scaled, misaligned, and lacks the correct colors.

labels.rationale: Figure A's labels are clear, readable, properly sized, and correctly positioned to match the reference, whereas Figure B's labels are extremely small, overlapping, and difficult to read.

concept.rationale: Figure B contains a fatal ReferenceError (calling a removed 'updateLabels' function), breaking the visualization, whereas Figure A provides a fully functional and accurate interactive reconstruction of the pedagogical concept.

aggregator.explanation: Figure A is the clear winner as it is fully functional, properly labeled, and accurately reproduces the visual layout and geometry of the original reference. In contrast, Figure B suffers from a fatal ReferenceError that breaks the visualization, rendering its theoretical interactivity enhancements unusable.

geometry.rationale: Figure A perfectly reconstructs the specific geometry, viewpoint, and MATLAB-style axis bounds and tick marks of the standard textbook binomial filter stem plot.

interactivity.rationale: Figure B provides a dynamic slider to change the filter order (regenerating the entire 3D plot and a live matrix overlay), offering significantly richer pedagogical exploration compared to Figure A's static order toggles.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure B captures the original camera angle, axis orientation, and layout much better than Figure A, despite some visual occlusion issues and extra UI elements.

labels.rationale: Figure B correctly assigns the 'm' and 'n' labels to the left and right axes respectively and places the z-axis label on the left, closely matching the reference, whereas Figure A swaps the axes and poorly places the z-axis label on the right.

concept.rationale: Figure A's addition of the outer product visualization perfectly illustrates the concept of filter separability, providing deeper pedagogical value for this specific concept.

aggregator.explanation: Figure B is the overall winner due to its significantly richer interactivity, offering a dynamic slider for pedagogical exploration, as well as superior faithfulness to the reference's layout, camera angle, and label placement. While Figure A provides excellent geometric reconstruction and conceptual illustration of separability, Figure B's comprehensive interactive features and accurate labeling give it the edge.

geometry.rationale: Figure A precisely reconstructs the specific aesthetic of a textbook 3D stem plot, using custom axis lines, floor/wall grids, and cylinder stems, rather than relying on generic ArrowHelpers like Figure B.

interactivity.rationale: Figure B offers interactive sliders to adjust the filter dimensions (N and M) along with a normalization toggle and a clickable step-through demonstration, whereas Figure A only allows toggling normalization on a static 3x3 filter.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure A accurately reproduces the axis limits, label placement, and grid structure of the original, whereas Figure B has incorrect limits, altered labels, and includes extraneous UI elements.

labels.rationale: Figure A correctly replicates the label text, tick values, and placement of the original, whereas Figure B has incorrect text, expanded axis ranges, and disjointed axis positioning.

concept.rationale: Figure A visually animates the change in stem height when the kernel is normalized, physically demonstrating the scaling concept, whereas Figure B normalizes the display height so the stems barely change size visually.

aggregator.explanation: Figure A significantly outperforms Figure B in reproducing the specific visual geometry, label accuracy, and axis constraints of the original plot. Furthermore, Figure A correctly animates the visual scaling concept when normalizing the kernel. Although Figure B provides more interactive UI elements, Figure A's superior visual fidelity and conceptual accuracy make it the clear overall winner.

geometry.rationale: Figure B correctly reconstructs the two-panel layout (k=1 and k=3) of the classic textbook figure and aligns the discrete time axis horizontally, which better matches the standard source perspective.

interactivity.rationale: Figure B features a highly pedagogical, custom-built interaction that links a cross-section plane and projection markers across two side-by-side 3D plots simultaneously.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure B faithfully replicates the side-by-side dual-plot composition, subfigure labels, and specific curve frequencies of the original, whereas Figure A only displays a single plot with extraneous vertical projection lines.

labels.rationale: Figure B correctly includes both plots with their respective '(a)' and '(b)' labels, and places axis labels like 'I', 'R', 'n', '20', and '40' much closer to their positions in the reference figure than Figure A.

concept.rationale: Figure B presents a highly effective side-by-side comparison (k=1 and k=3) with a synchronizing cross-sectional plane that lets students interactive trace the same discrete sample 'n' across both frequencies, explicitly illustrating the core comparative concept.

aggregator.explanation: Figure B is overwhelmingly preferred across all dimensions due to its faithful side-by-side reconstruction of the original two-panel layout and accurate labeling. Additionally, it incorporates a highly pedagogical interactive cross-section plane that synchronizes across both plots, whereas Figure A only displays a single plot.
